attractions.name: Fiesta Noche del Rio
attractions.description: Fiesta Noche del Rio is a vibrant cultural extravaganza and the oldest outdoor dance performance of its kind in the United States, celebrating San Antonio's rich heritage through song and dance. Performed at the historic Arneson River Theatre, the show features authentic Mexican, Spanish, and Texas performances by local professionals. Alamo Kiwanis Club, Fiesta Noche del Rio
attractions.address: 418 Villita St, San Antonio, TX 78205 Arneson River Theatre
attractions.region: La Villita / Downtown San Antonio Get Creative San Antonio
attractions.hours: Friday: 8:30 PM, Saturday: 8:30 PM, Sunday-Thursday: Closed (Seasonal performance schedule) Fiesta Noche del Rio
attractions.days_open: Friday, Saturday (Seasonal: June 5 - August 8) Fiesta Noche del Rio
attractions.admission: Adults: $25.00, Seniors (60+): $20.00, Children (6-14): $10.00, Children under 6: Free, Active & Retired Military: $20.00, Groups (10-25): $18.00/person, Groups (26+): $15.00/person Fiesta Noche del Rio Tickets
highlights: Authentic flamenco guitar and traditional Spanish dance performances Fiesta Noche del Rio Gallery
Vibrant Mexican folk dances featuring colorful, traditional costumes Fiesta Noche del Rio
A grand finale featuring a live mariachi band performance Tripadvisor Reviews
Interactive audience participation and entertaining hosts KSAT News
Spectacular riverside setting at the historic Arneson River Theatre Wikipedia
Charitable mission with all proceeds benefiting local children's charities Alamo Kiwanis Club
attractions.tips: Arrive by 8:00 PM to secure the best seating as it is first-come, first-served; bring a cushion or blanket for the grass and stone amphitheater seating; if you take a late-evening river cruise, you can catch a glimpse of the performance from the water. Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Hemisfair
attractions.description: Hemisfair is a vibrant urban district in the heart of downtown San Antonio, redeveloping the site of the 1968 World's Fair into a sustainable and walkable neighborhood. It currently features three distinct public parks—Yanaguana Garden, Civic Park, and Tower Park—offering a blend of world-class play areas, lush green spaces, and historic preservation. The district serves as a community gathering place where visitors can enjoy local dining, cultural events, and the iconic Tower of the Americas (Hemisfair, Wikipedia).
attractions.address: 434 S. Alamo St.
attractions.hours: Monday: 5:00 AM - Midnight; Tuesday: 5:00 AM - Midnight; Wednesday: 5:00 AM - Midnight; Thursday: 5:00 AM - Midnight; Friday: 5:00 AM - Midnight; Saturday: 5:00 AM - Midnight; Sunday: 5:00 AM - Midnight (Hemisfair)
attractions.admission: Free (Hemisfair, Tour Texas)
highlights: Yanaguana Garden: A vibrant 4-acre park featuring a playground, splash pad, sand play, and climbing structures (Hemisfair).
Civic Park: A large community space with a 10,000-person capacity great lawn and "The Springs" water features (Hemisfair).
Tower of the Americas: A 750-foot observation tower offering panoramic views of the city and a revolving restaurant (Wikipedia).
Historic Preservation: The district incorporates several restored 19th-century homes and structures from the 1968 World's Fair (Hemisfair).
Mexican Cultural Institute: A venue within the park that offers free exhibits and performances celebrating Mexican heritage (Visit San Antonio).
Local Dining & Shopping: Home to various local favorites like Dough Pizzeria Napoletana, CommonWealth Coffeehouse, and Lick Honest Ice Creams (Hemisfair).
attractions.tips: Pets are welcome at Hemisfair but must remain leashed; they are allowed in Civic Park's springs but prohibited from the Yanaguana splash pad. The Union Pacific Railroad Splash Pad in Yanaguana Garden is open seasonally from 9:00 AM to 9:00 PM. Parking is available in several nearby lots, though rates vary based on special events (Hemisfair).
attractions.nearby: Tower of the Americas, The Alamo, San Antonio River Walk, Henry B. González Convention Center, La Villita Historic Arts Village (Expedia)

attractions.name: Lila Cockrell Theatre
attractions.description: The Lila Cockrell Theatre is a premier performing arts venue located within the Henry B. González Convention Center complex in San Antonio, Texas. Named after the city's first female mayor, it was originally constructed for the 1968 World's Fair (HemisFair '68) and serves as a versatile space for ballet, symphony, concerts, and major convention gatherings. Official Site
attractions.address: 900 E. Market Street
attractions.hours: Mon-Sun: 7:00 AM - 12:30 AM (Daily hours vary by event; Box Office open day of show only) Waze, Ticketmaster
attractions.admission: Varies by event; tickets available via Ticketmaster Official Site
highlights: Named after Lila Cockrell, the first female mayor of San Antonio Official Site
Features the monumental 'Confluence of Civilizations in the Americas' mosaic mural by Juan O'Gorman San Antonio River Authority
Boasts a seating capacity of 2,319 across three elegant levels Official Site
Integral part of the historic 1968 San Antonio World's Fair legacy GPSmyCity
Ideally situated on the east bank of the famous San Antonio River Walk Official Site
Renovated in 2010 to feature modern acoustics and multimedia capabilities GPSmyCity
attractions.tips: Parking is recommended at the Riverbend, Marina, or Rivercenter Mall garages as building parking may be limited. Arrive at least 90 minutes before showtime as doors typically open early for security and concessions. Don't miss the Juan O'Gorman mural on the exterior facade, best viewed from the River Walk. Ticketmaster, Texas Standard
attractions.nearby: Henry B. González Convention Center, Tower of the Americas, San Antonio River Walk, Hemisfair Park, The Alamo, Shops at Rivercenter GPSmyCity

attractions.name: Mi Tierra Café y Panadería
attractions.description: Established in 1941 by Pedro and Cruz Cortez, Mi Tierra Café y Panadería is a legendary San Antonio institution located in the heart of the historic Market Square. This world-famous landmark is renowned for its vibrant, year-round festive atmosphere, authentic Tex-Mex cuisine, and a massive 24-hour panadería offering traditional Mexican pastries and dulces. Official Website, Visit San Antonio
attractions.address: 218 Produce Row, San Antonio, TX 78207
attractions.region: Historic Market Square / Downtown San Antonio
attractions.hours: Sun: 8:00 AM - 10:00 PM; Mon: 8:00 AM - 10:00 PM; Tue: 8:00 AM - 10:00 PM; Wed: 8:00 AM - 10:00 PM; Thu: 8:00 AM - 10:00 PM; Fri: 8:00 AM - 11:00 PM; Sat: 8:00 AM - 11:00 PM. TripAdvisor, Instagram
attractions.admission: Free admission; food and bakery items for purchase. Visit San Antonio
highlights: The "American Dream" mural in the back dining room, featuring over 100 influential Latino leaders and family members. Texas Highways
A sprawling on-site panadería (bakery) offering a wide selection of traditional Mexican pastries like pan dulce and campechenas. Official Website
Year-round festive decor including colorful papel picado and thousands of sparkling Christmas lights. San Antonio Report
Live entertainment from strolling mariachi trios that serenade diners throughout the restaurant. Wanderlog
Authentic Tex-Mex classics served in large portions, including their famous chilaquiles and enchiladas. TripAdvisor
attractions.tips: Expect significant wait times during weekends and peak dining hours, as the restaurant does not typically take reservations. Visit at off-peak times or visit the bakery counter directly if you are short on time. Be sure to explore the back rooms to view the iconic murals. Wanderlog, TripAdvisor
attractions.nearby: Historic Market Square (El Mercado), San Fernando Cathedral, Milam Park, Spanish Governor's Palace. TripAdvisor

attractions.name: The Alamo (Alamo Mission in San Antonio)
attractions.description: The Alamo is a historic Spanish mission and fortress compound that served as the site of the pivotal Battle of the Alamo in 1836. Today, it stands as a revered symbol of Texas liberty, featuring the iconic Alamo Church, the Long Barrack museum, and extensive gardens in the heart of downtown San Antonio. thealamo.org, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 300 Alamo Plaza, San Antonio, TX 78205
attractions.region: Alamo Plaza Historic District / Downtown San Antonio
attractions.hours: Open Daily: 9 a.m. – 5:30 p.m. (Last entry 30 minutes prior to closing); Collections Center: 9 a.m. – 5 p.m. (Last entry 1 hour prior to closing); Plaza de Valero: Sunday–Thursday 6 a.m. – 10 p.m., Friday–Saturday 6 a.m. – 11 p.m. thealamo.org
attractions.admission: Alamo Church: Free (timed-entry reservation required); Guided Tour: $48 Adult, $38 Child (12 and under); Audio Tour & Exhibit: $20 Adult, $18 Child; Alamo Exhibit Only: $14 Adult, $10 Child; Surrounded! Augmented Reality: $10 Adult ($5 with paid experience) thealamo.org
highlights: The iconic Alamo Church, a 300-year-old former mission and shrine.
The Long Barrack, the oldest building on the site housing a museum of the Texas Revolution.
The Phil Collins Collection, featuring hundreds of artifacts donated by the famous musician.
Live history demonstrations including musket firing and 1830s daily life reenactments.
The 18-Pounder Cannon replica, representing the largest cannon used during the 1836 siege.
The Alamo Cenotaph, a monument in the plaza honoring the defenders who died in the battle.
attractions.tips: Reserve your free timed-entry tickets for the Alamo Church in advance as they often sell out. Men should remove hats when entering the Church, and photography is prohibited inside the historic structures. Wear comfortable shoes and bring water as most of the 4.2-acre site is outdoors. thealamo.org
attractions.nearby: San Antonio River Walk, San Antonio River Walk, Ripley's Believe It or Not!, The Buckhorn Saloon and Texas Ranger Museum, San Fernando Cathedral, La Villita Historic Arts Village, Menger Hotel, Tower of the Americas. Tripadvisor, Amazing Mazes

attractions.name: The Buckhorn Saloon & Museum and Texas Ranger Museum
attractions.description: The Buckhorn Saloon & Museum is a historic San Antonio landmark featuring an extensive collection of over 520 species of wildlife and the comprehensive Texas Ranger Museum. Established in 1881, it remains a working saloon where visitors can explore themed halls of curiosities while enjoying a drink in the same spot where Teddy Roosevelt once recruited Rough Riders. (Official Site, Authentic Texas)
attractions.address: 318 E. Houston Street
attractions.hours: Museums, Saloon, and Gift Shop: Open daily at 10:00 AM (Closing times vary; call 210-247-4012 for exact times). Buckhorn Burger Company: 11:00 AM - 3:00 PM daily. (Official Site)
attractions.admission: Adults (12+): $22.99 + tax; Children (3-11): $16.99 + tax; Children (2 and under): Free (Official Site)
highlights: Hall of Horns featuring over 520 species of wildlife from every continent.
Texas Ranger Museum with hundreds of authentic badges, handguns, and historical artifacts.
Historic Saloon featuring the original cherry wood and marble back-bar from the 1880s.
'Rangertown,' a detailed recreation of turn-of-the-century San Antonio including a jail cell and blacksmith shop.
The '78-point Buck,' a world-famous non-typical whitetail deer mount.
Carnival of Curiosities and American Sideshow exhibits featuring mysterious and mind-boggling oddities.
attractions.tips: Admission tickets include entry to both the Buckhorn Museum and the Texas Ranger Museum; however, no ticket is required to visit the saloon, cafe, or gift shop. Visitors are permitted to purchase a drink at the saloon and carry it with them while touring the museum exhibits. (Official Site, YouTube)
attractions.nearby: The Alamo, San Antonio Riverwalk, San Antonio Museum of Art, Witte Museum, Tower of the Americas (Tour Texas, GetYourGuide)

attractions.name: The Majestic Theatre
attractions.description: The Majestic Theatre is San Antonio's oldest and largest atmospheric theatre, designed in 1929 as a stunning 2,264-seat venue. A National Historic Landmark, it features a Spanish Mediterranean interior with a night-sky ceiling filled with twinkling stars and floating clouds (Wikipedia), ATG Tickets).
attractions.address: 224 E Houston St, San Antonio, TX 78205
attractions.hours: Phone Hours: Sunday - Saturday, 8:00 AM - 8:00 PM CT; Box Office: Open on show dates only, 3 hours prior to ticketed time (2 hours for shows at 1:00 PM or earlier) (ATG Tickets).
attractions.days_open: Open for scheduled performances and tours; Box office is open show dates only, starting three hours prior to performance (ATG Tickets).
attractions.admission: Ticket prices vary by event, typically ranging from $10 to over $140 for Broadway shows and concerts; Historic Venue Tours are available for approximately $30.00 plus service fees (Vivid Seats, ATG Tickets).
attractions.phone: 1 (888) 811-5040 (Customer Services) or (210) 226-5700 (ATG Tickets, Historic Theatre Photos)
highlights: Atmospheric 'starry sky' ceiling with cloud projections and twinkling lights (Wikipedia))
Intricate Spanish Mediterranean architecture and ornate plasterwork (San Antonio Report)
Home to the Broadway in San Antonio series and diverse live performances (ATG Tickets)
First fully air-conditioned theatre in Texas (Texas State Historical Association)
Designated National Historic Landmark and Texas Historic Landmark (Wikipedia))
Features a unique collection of stuffed birds, including a signature white peacock (San Antonio Report)
attractions.tips: Arrive at least an hour early to appreciate the lobby architecture and avoid traffic bottlenecks; parking can be reserved in advance at nearby garages like the Mid-City or Travis Park Plaza to save time and money (Reddit, ATG Tickets).
attractions.nearby: Charline McCombs Empire Theatre, The Espee, San Antonio River Walk, Aztec Theatre (ATG Tickets, Expedia)

attractions.name: Louis Tussaud's Waxworks San Antonio
attractions.description: Louis Tussaud’s Waxworks in San Antonio features over 200 lifelike wax figures ranging from Hollywood celebrities to historical world leaders. Located directly across from the Alamo, the museum offers 16 themed galleries where visitors can pose for photos with their favorite stars. It provides a climate-controlled, family-friendly environment that blends entertainment with the artistry of wax sculpting Ripley's Official Site.
attractions.address: 301 Alamo Plaza, San Antonio, TX 78205
attractions.hours: Mon-Wed: 10:00 a.m. – 8:00 p.m., Thu: 10:00 a.m. – 8:00 p.m. (May close earlier for private events), Fri-Sat: 10:00 a.m. – 11:00 p.m., Sun: 10:00 a.m. – 8:00 p.m. Official Site Hours
attractions.admission: Adult (13+): $24.99, Child (4-12): $19.99. Multi-attraction combo tickets are available (Save $15-$40 per person) Official Website
highlights: Over 200 lifelike wax figures including celebrities and historical icons
16 themed galleries like Hollywood Legends and Sports Stars
Interactive exhibits where visitors can pose with DC and Marvel superheroes
A spine-tingling Chamber of Horrors section
Historical displays featuring U.S. Presidents and World Leaders
attractions.tips: Buy a combo ticket to save on nearby Ripley's attractions. Bring your camera as photos and selfies with the figures are highly encouraged. Visit on a weekday or early in the morning to avoid the largest crowds Wonderful Museums.
attractions.nearby: The Alamo, San Antonio River Walk, Ripley’s Believe It or Not! Odditorium, Ripley’s 4D Moving Theater, The Shops at Rivercenter Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Biga on the Banks
attractions.description: Biga on the Banks is a premiere contemporary-regional fine dining restaurant located on the San Antonio River Walk, led by eight-time James Beard Award nominee Chef Bruce Auden. The restaurant is known for its "New American" cuisine, featuring a daily-changing menu that blends worldly influences with fresh, local ingredients in a relaxed, elegant atmosphere. Biga Official Site, Tour Texas

Sweeping views of a quiet, cypress-shaded bend of the San Antonio River Walk Tour Texas

Highly-rated dining experience with multiple Zagat and Diner’s Choice honors Biga Official Site

attractions.name: Shops at Rivercenter
attractions.description: The Shops at Rivercenter is a premier multi-level shopping, dining, and entertainment destination located in the heart of downtown San Antonio. Uniquely integrated with the world-famous River Walk, the complex features over 100 retailers, 14 full-service restaurants, and a 1,001-room Marriott hotel. Shops at Rivercenter, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 849 E. Commerce St, San Antonio, TX 78205
attractions.hours: Monday – Saturday: 10:00 A.M. – 8:00 P.M.; Sunday: 12:00 P.M. – 6:00 P.M. Shops at Rivercenter
attractions.admission: Free (Individual attractions like LEGOLAND and SEA LIFE require tickets) Shops at Rivercenter

Over 100 retail stores including H&M and American Eagle
Major attractions like LEGOLAND Discovery Center and SEA LIFE Aquarium
Entertainment options including AMC Theatres, IMAX, and Dave & Buster's
Diverse dining options from casual food court bites to waterfront full-service restaurants
Historic Joske's building integration providing a blend of old-world charm and modern retail
attractions.tips: Use the mall as an air-conditioned shortcut between the Alamo and the River Walk. Visit during weekday mornings to avoid peak tourist crowds. Many restaurants offer parking validation, so be sure to ask if you are driving. Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Texas Cavaliers River Parade
attractions.description: The Texas Cavaliers River Parade is a one-of-a-kind floating celebration featuring over 45 vibrantly decorated floats that glide along the San Antonio River (Texas Cavaliers). Established in 1941, this historic event draws over 250,000 spectators to the River Walk and raises millions of dollars for local children's charities (Fiesta San Antonio).
attractions.address: San Antonio River Walk (starting near Navarro St & N Main Ave), San Antonio, TX 78205
attractions.hours: Typically 7:00 PM – 9:00 PM; Gates/Doors for dining often open at 5:00 PM or 6:00 PM (Texas Cavaliers, Joe's Crab Shack)
attractions.days_open: Annually on the first Monday of Fiesta (typically in April) (Texas Cavaliers)
attractions.admission: Individual tickets: $29.00, $33.00, $35.00; VIP restaurant packages: $155.00+ (Etix, Saltgrass Steak House)
highlights: Over 45 decorated boats representing schools, businesses, and civic groups (Texas Cavaliers)
Appearances by King Antonio and the Grand Marshal to lead the festivities (Texas Cavaliers)
Live music ranging from festive bands to traditional mariachis on the water (Texas Cavaliers)
The "Show us your shoes" tradition where participants reveal unique footwear (Texas Highways)
All net proceeds benefit more than 60 local children's charities (Texas Cavaliers)
attractions.tips: Arrive 1-2 hours early to navigate heavy downtown traffic and secure parking near Navarro or Houston Street (Joe's Crab Shack). Wear comfortable shoes for the River Walk's cobblestones and bring light, breezy clothing for the humid April weather (Texas Highways). The parade is a smoke-free event, and glass containers are strictly prohibited (Texas Cavaliers).
attractions.nearby: The Alamo, Briscoe Western Art Museum, Shops at Rivercenter, Henry B. Gonzalez Convention Center, La Villita Historic Arts Village (The Alamo, The San Antonio River Walk)

attractions.name: The Briscoe Western Art Museum
attractions.description: The Briscoe Western Art Museum preserves and interprets the art, history, and culture of the American West. Located on the historic San Antonio River Walk in a building that originally served as the city's first public library, the museum features a wide array of paintings, sculptures, and artifacts that tell the stories of cowboys, Native Americans, and the diverse cultures of the region. The Briscoe Western Art Museum, Tripadvisor
attractions.address: 210 W. Market Street, San Antonio, TX 78205 The Briscoe Western Art Museum
attractions.hours: Monday: 10:00am–5:00pm; Tuesday: Closed; Wednesday: Closed; Thursday: 10:00am–8:00pm; Friday: 10:00am–5:00pm; Saturday: 10:00am–5:00pm; Sunday: 10:00am–5:00pm The Briscoe Western Art Museum

Santa Anna's Sword and artifacts from the Texas Revolution Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Tobin Center for the Performing Arts
attractions.description: The Tobin Center for the Performing Arts is a world-class venue that masterfully blends the historic 1920s Municipal Auditorium with cutting-edge technology and modern architecture. As a non-profit cultural hub, it hosts a diverse range of performances from resident companies like Opera San Antonio and Ballet San Antonio to international touring acts. LMN Architects, LinkedIn
attractions.address: 100 Auditorium Circle, San Antonio, TX 78205
attractions.hours: Monday - Friday: 10 am - 6 pm; Saturday: 10 am - 2 pm; Performance Days: Open 2 hours prior to show time. Box Office
attractions.admission: Varies by performance (tickets starting from ~$33.50); Children 1 year and under may use lap seating (may be subject to a charge). Box Office
highlights: Incorporates the historic 1926 Spanish Colonial Revival façade of the San Antonio Municipal Auditorium. LMN Architects
Features a state-of-the-art automated floor system in the H-E-B Performance Hall that can transition from raked seating to a flat floor in minutes. LMN Architects
Home to ten resident companies, including Ballet San Antonio, Opera San Antonio, and Youth Orchestras of San Antonio. Tobin Center
Includes the H-E-B Performance Hall (1,768 seats), Carlos Alvarez Studio Theater (black box), and the outdoor Will Naylor Smith River Walk Plaza. Wikipedia
Recognized for its world-class acoustics and intimate design, with no seat in the main hall further than 150 feet from the stage. The Architect's Newspaper
attractions.tips: Use the Noble Bar App to pre-order concessions and skip lines during intermission. Members have exclusive access to the Denman Founders Lounge for private bar service prior to performances. Check the 'Know Before You Go' guide for updated security and prohibited item policies. Dining & Concessions, Know Before You Go

attractions.name: First Friday Art Walk
attractions.description: Established in 1994, First Friday is San Antonio’s longest-running monthly art event anchored by the Blue Star Arts Complex. It features open artist studios, local galleries, live music, and a vibrant street market throughout the Southtown neighborhood (Blue Star Arts Complex, The Paisano).
attractions.address: 1414 S Alamo St
attractions.hours: 6:00 PM – 11:59 PM (on First Fridays) (Blue Star Arts Complex)
attractions.days_open: First Friday of every month (Blue Star Arts Complex, Instagram)
attractions.admission: Free (Facebook, Instagram)
highlights: Over 20 open art galleries and studios to explore (KSAT)
Live music performances and local art vendors (Blue Star Arts Complex)
Wide variety of food trucks and local dining options in Southtown (Instagram)
Vibrant neighborhood atmosphere spanning the King William area (Instagram)
Pop-up markets featuring vintage clothes and handmade goods (Reddit)
attractions.tips: Arrive early to find parking as it gets very crowded. Consider visiting the contemporary gallery first as it may close earlier than other spaces. Bringing cash is recommended for purchasing from local vendors (Reddit).
attractions.nearby: King William Historic District, San Antonio River Walk, Blue Star Contemporary Art Museum (Instagram, Reddit)

attractions.name: King William Historic District
attractions.description: The King William Historic District is a 25-block neighborhood south of downtown San Antonio, originally settled by wealthy German immigrants in the late 1800s. Known as Texas' first residential historic district, it features an opulent collection of Greek Revival, Italianate, and Victorian mansions along shaded streets bordering the San Antonio River. (King William Association, Texas State Historical Association)
attractions.address: 122 Madison St. (Association Office), Southtown Area
attractions.hours: Open daily 24/7 for neighborhood walking; Villa Finale and Steves Homestead have specific museum hours (typically 10 AM - 4 PM) (Texas Time Travel, Tripadvisor)
attractions.admission: Free (Public district), guided tours range from $20 to $180+ (Viator)
highlights: Collection of stunning 19th-century mansions including the Steves Homestead and Villa Finale (Viator)
Beautiful walking paths along the Mission Reach of the San Antonio River Walk (ForeverVacation)
Annual King William Fair, a major event of Fiesta San Antonio featuring parades and arts (Fiesta San Antonio)
Access to the historical Blue Star Arts Complex and local art galleries (Visit San Antonio)
The historic Guenther House, located at the site of the Pioneer Flour Mills (Tripadvisor)
attractions.tips: Wear comfortable walking shoes as the best way to see the district is on foot or by bike; download the free King William walking tour app for audio history; visit in April for the King William Fair. (King William Association, Facebook)
attractions.nearby: The Alamo, Tower of the Americas, Blue Star Arts Complex, Briscoe Western Art Museum, and the San Antonio River Walk (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: South Alamo Street (Historic District)
attractions.description: South Alamo Street is a historic and vibrant corridor that serves as a bridge between San Antonio's downtown and the eclectic Southtown arts district. Renowned for its diverse architectural styles and cultural significance, the street is a hub for local art galleries, historic landmarks, and some of the city's most beloved culinary destinations. (Visit San Antonio, Texas Highways)

attractions.category: Arts District / Neighborhood Visit San Antonio, Wikipedia
attractions.description: Southtown is a vibrant arts and culture district located just south of downtown San Antonio, encompassing five historic neighborhoods including King William and Lavaca. Known for its eclectic energy, the area features a blend of Victorian architecture, contemporary art galleries, and diverse culinary hotspots. It is a hub for the local creative community, anchored by the Blue Star Arts Complex and celebrated through monthly art walks. Visit San Antonio, The San Antonio River Walk

attractions.admission: Free Visit San Antonio, Blue Star Arts

attractions.name: Cured at Pearl
attractions.description: Cured at Pearl is a renowned gastro-pub celebrated for its artisanal house-cured meats and seasonally dynamic menus. Housed in a historic 1904 building that once served as the Pearl Brewery's administrative offices, the restaurant reflects Chef Steve McHugh’s commitment to regional ingredients and traditional preservation methods. Its centerpiece is a massive humidity-controlled glass locker, known as the 'Charc Tank,' where various meats and salamis are aged to perfection.
attractions.address: 306 Pearl Parkway, Suite 101, San Antonio, TX 78215
attractions.hours: Monday - Thursday: 3:00 PM - 10:00 PM; Friday: 3:00 PM - 11:00 PM; Saturday: 10:00 AM - 11:00 PM; Sunday: 10:00 AM - 9:00 PM (Note: Restaurant reported closed January 2026)

attractions.admission: Free (Restaurant)
highlights: Features a prominent 'Charc Tank' displaying artisanal meats aged from 30 days to 12 months.
Located in the historic 1904 Pearl Brewery Administration Building, preserving original wood floors and brick.
Led by Chef Steve McHugh, a six-time James Beard Foundation Award finalist.
Employs a '60-mile' sourcing philosophy, focusing on the purest regional ingredients.
Offers a unique 'Mountain Mary' brunch cocktail made with moonshine and pickled okra.
Recognized by Bon Appétit and Esquire as one of America's best new restaurants upon its opening.
attractions.tips: Arrive during the daily Happy Hour (3:00 PM - 6:00 PM) for exceptional deals on the famous $6 burger and fried quail legs. For a complete experience, order the 'Chef's Selection' charcuterie board to taste a variety of house-made specialties. Reservations are highly recommended for dinner and weekend brunch as the venue is a local staple. Despite the closure announcement, check for potential pop-ups or new ventures from Chef McHugh in the San Antonio area.

It is celebrated for its remarkably preserved original frescoes and its status as a key site within the San Antonio Missions UNESCO World Heritage Site (Wikipedia).

The facility currently serves as the home field for the UTSA Roadrunners football team and the San Antonio Brahmas of the UFL Alamodome Official Site.

attractions.nearby: San Antonio River Walk and The Alamo, San Marcos Premium Outlets, Canyon Lake, Wimberley (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Comal River (City of New Braunfels)
attractions.description: The Comal River is a spring-fed waterway known as the 'longest shortest river in the world,' flowing 2.5 miles through the heart of New Braunfels. It offers a popular tubing experience with a constant 72-degree temperature year-round and features a thrilling city-managed tube chute (New Braunfels Official Site, American Trails).
attractions.address: 100 Liebscher, New Braunfels, TX 78130
attractions.region: Downtown New Braunfels / Texas Hill Country (Tour Texas)
attractions.hours: River Access Parks: 6:00 AM - 12:00 AM daily; City Tube Chute: 10:00 AM - 7:00 PM (seasonal hours apply) (New Braunfels Official Site)
attractions.days_open: Daily for recreation; Tube rentals available Saturdays & Sundays in May and mid-August to Labor Day, daily from Memorial Day to mid-August (New Braunfels Official Site)
attractions.admission: $15 tube rental + $20 refundable deposit (City Tube Chute), $2 River Activity Wristband (weekends/holidays), $20 non-resident vehicle parking (New Braunfels Official Site)
highlights: The City Tube Chute provides an exciting rapid section for adventurous floaters. (New Braunfels Official Site)
The river maintains a consistent 72-degree temperature throughout the year. (Tour Texas)
At 2.5 miles long, it is one of the shortest navigable rivers in the world. (American Trails)
Visitors can rent tubes directly from the City at Prince Solms Park or various private outfitters. (New Braunfels Official Site)
The river flows past major landmarks like Landa Park and Schlitterbahn Waterpark. (Tour Texas)
attractions.tips: Arrival before 11:00 AM is highly recommended to secure parking, especially on weekends. Follow the 'New Braunfels Can Ban' rule: no disposable containers are allowed on the river, so use reusable bottles and containers. All coolers must be 30 quarts or smaller and have a locking mechanism. (New Braunfels Official Site, Texas Tubes)
attractions.nearby: Landa Park, Schlitterbahn Waterpark, Historic Gruene District, Wurstfest Grounds (Tour Texas)

attractions.name: Dwight D. Eisenhower Park
attractions.description: Dwight D. Eisenhower Park is a 420-acre natural area park situated in the scenic Texas Hill Country, featuring rocky canyons, wooded dry creek beds, and diverse landscapes. It offers over 6 miles of both paved and unpaved hiking trails and is home to a variety of local wildlife, including white-tail deer and armadillos. The park's centerpiece is a 1,280-foot observation tower that provides panoramic views of the San Antonio skyline and the surrounding region.
attractions.address: 19399 NW Military Hwy., San Antonio, TX 78257
attractions.hours: Sunday - Saturday: Sunrise - Sunset
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: 1,280-foot observation tower with stunning views of downtown San Antonio and the Hill Country.
Over 6 miles of diverse hiking trails, ranging from easy paved paths to challenging rocky terrain.
Direct connection to the Leon Creek Greenway and Salado Creek Greenway trail systems.
Abundant wildlife viewing opportunities for birdwatching and spotting deer or armadillos.
Family-friendly amenities including a playground and multiple picnic pavilions with BBQ grills.
Designated natural area focused on preserving the local ecosystem and Hill Country beauty.
attractions.tips: Arrive early on weekends to secure a parking spot as the lot fills up quickly. Note that bicycles, rollerblades, and skateboards are strictly prohibited on the trails to protect the natural area. Be sure to stay on designated trails to preserve the sensitive environment, and bring plenty of water for the more challenging, unpaved hikes. Leashed dogs are welcome, but owners must clean up after them.
attractions.nearby: The Rim Shopping Center, The Shops at La Cantera, Six Flags Fiesta Texas, Friedrich Wilderness Park, Crownridge Canyon Park

attractions.name: Fiesta® San Antonio
attractions.description: Fiesta San Antonio is an annual 11-day citywide celebration that began in 1891 to honor the heroes of the Alamo and the Battle of San Jacinto. It has evolved into one of the nation's premier festivals, featuring over 100 diverse events including massive parades, live music, and cultural food fairs that attract over 2.5 million participants. Visit San Antonio, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 110 Broadway, Suite 350, San Antonio, TX 78205
attractions.hours: Commission Office: Mon–Thu 8:30 AM – 5:00 PM, Fri 8:30 AM – 12:00 PM (Extended in Mar/Apr: Mon–Fri 8:00 AM – 6:00 PM); Festival events typically run 10:00 AM – 11:00 PM daily. Fiesta San Antonio, KSAT
attractions.days_open: Annually for 11 days in April; Commission office open Monday-Friday. Fiesta San Antonio
attractions.admission: Varies by event. Many events are free (e.g., Fiesta Fiesta, Chaparral Music Festival); major ticketed events include A Night in Old San Antonio ($20 online/$25 gate), Battle of Flowers Parade ($16.95-$33), and Fiesta de los Reyes ($5 peak fee for ages 13+). TPR, KSAT
highlights: Battle of Flowers® Parade - The founding floral parade and oldest part of the event. Visit San Antonio
Fiesta Flambeau® Parade - The largest illuminated night parade in the United States. Fiesta San Antonio
A Night in Old San Antonio (NIOSA) - A four-night historic block party in La Villita. TPR
Texas Cavaliers River Parade - A unique floating parade featuring decorated boats on the River Walk. TPR
Fiesta Carnival - A multi-day event with rides and games held at the Alamodome parking lot. TPR
St. Mary's University Fiesta Oyster Bake - A popular two-day music and food festival. Wikipedia
attractions.tips: Wear bright colors and comfortable shoes; traditional Latin Guayabera shirts and flower crowns are popular. Collect and trade 'Fiesta Medals' to participate in a local tradition. Use VIA Park & Ride to avoid heavy downtown traffic. Fiesta San Antonio, Visit San Antonio
attractions.nearby: The Alamo, San Antonio River Walk, Historic Market Square, La Villita Historic Arts Village, San Fernando Cathedral. Trot Op!

attractions.name: Freetail Brewing Company
attractions.description: Freetail Brewing Co. is a local staple that has been brewing innovative craft beer and serving scratch-made pizza since 2008. The brewery draws inspiration from the Mexican Free-tailed bat and the vibrant culture of San Antonio to create unique flavors like La Muerta and Tropical Punch Bexarliner (Freetail Brewing Co.).
attractions.address: 4035 North Loop 1604 West, Suite 105, San Antonio, TX 78257
attractions.hours: Thursday - Saturday: 11:00 AM - 11:00 PM; Sunday - Wednesday: 11:00 AM - 10:00 PM (Freetail Brewing Co.)
attractions.admission: Free (Freetail Brewing Co.)
highlights: Extensive selection of house-brewed ales, lagers, sours, and stouts (Freetail Brewing Co.)
Hand-crafted artisan pizzas made with scratch-made dough (Yelp)
Spacious outdoor patio area that is dog-friendly (Tripadvisor)
Weekly community events including trivia, live music, and yoga (Freetail Brewing Co.)
Interactive beer flights allowing guests to sample multiple styles (Tripadvisor)
attractions.tips: It is highly recommended to make a reservation online before visiting to ensure table availability. Order a beer flight to experience their diverse range of sours and seasonal releases, and don't miss the brisket queso (Freetail Brewing Co., Tripadvisor).
attractions.nearby: Friedrich Wilderness Park, The Escape Game San Antonio, iFLY Indoor Skydiving, Eisenhower Park (Tripadvisor, Yelp)

attractions.name: Friedrich Wilderness Park
attractions.description: Emilie and Albert Friedrich Wilderness Park is a 600-acre natural area located on the Balcones Escarpment at the southern edge of the Texas Hill Country. The park features approximately 10 miles of hiking trails ranging from easy paved paths to challenging rocky climbs with scenic canyon vistas. It is internationally recognized for bird watching, serving as a critical nesting site for the endangered Golden-cheeked Warbler and Black-capped Vireo. City of San Antonio, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 21395 Milsa Dr.
attractions.hours: Sunday - Saturday: Sunrise - Sunset City of San Antonio
attractions.admission: Free City of San Antonio
highlights: Approximately 10 miles of hiking trails with varying degrees of difficulty
Nesting sites for endangered Golden-cheeked Warblers and Black-capped Vireos
Handicapped accessible paved nature trails and segments
Stunning Hill Country views from the highest points in the park
Native Plant Society Wildscape and butterfly garden area
600 acres of undeveloped terrain featuring steep hills and deep canyons
attractions.tips: Pets are strictly prohibited to protect sensitive wildlife; parking is limited and often fills by 8:00 AM on weekends; water is only available at the trailhead; wear sturdy shoes for rocky unpaved trails. Friends of San Antonio Natural Areas, Tripadvisor
attractions.nearby: Crownridge Canyon Natural Area, Eisenhower Park, Six Flags Fiesta Texas, The Shops at La Cantera, Leon Springs Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Frost Bank Center (formerly AT&T Center)
attractions.description: The Frost Bank Center, formerly known as the AT&T Center, is a premier multi-purpose entertainment venue in South Texas and home to the San Antonio Spurs (NBA). Opened in 2002 and renovated in 2015, the 18,000-plus seat arena also hosts the San Antonio Stock Show & Rodeo along with numerous concerts and special events annually (Official Website).
attractions.address: 1 Frost Bank Center Drive, San Antonio, TX 78219 (Official Website)
attractions.region: Eastside / Arena District (San Antonio Report, Reddit)
attractions.hours: Box Office: Open on event days only, starting 3 hours prior to event start time; Administrative/Service team: Monday–Friday, 8:30 AM – 5:30 PM (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Prices vary by event; average ticket price is approximately $83 (Vivid Seats); children 2 years and older require a ticket (Ticketmaster)
highlights: Home to the five-time NBA champion San Antonio Spurs (Wikipedia)
Host of the annual San Antonio Stock Show & Rodeo (Official Website)
Multi-purpose facility for major concerts, family shows, and sporting events (Official Website)
Underwent a $110+ million renovation in 2015 to enhance fan experience (Official Website)
Features premium seating options like Terrace Suites and Courtside Boxes (Official Website)
Cashless venue supporting mobile and card payments for all transactions (Official Website)
attractions.tips: Arrive early as doors typically open one hour before the event; the venue is entirely cashless, so bring a credit/debit card or use mobile payment. Be aware of the strict bag policy: only small bags up to 6\"x8\" are permitted (Official Website). Pre-purchasing parking is recommended for easier access on event nights (Official Website).
attractions.nearby: Freeman Coliseum, San Antonio River Walk (approx. 3 miles), The Alamo (approx. 3 miles), Willow Springs Golf Course (Tripadvisor, San Antonio Report)

attractions.name: Frost Bank Center Official Website
attractions.description: The Frost Bank Center is a premier multi-purpose indoor arena that serves as the home for the NBA's San Antonio Spurs and the annual San Antonio Stock Show & Rodeo. Opened in 2002, the venue hosts a wide range of events including concerts, professional wrestling, and monster truck shows, seating up to 19,000 guests. Wikipedia, Official Website
attractions.address: 1 Frost Bank Center Drive, San Antonio, TX 78219, US Ticketmaster
attractions.region: Eastside (Arena District) Yelp, Visit San Antonio
attractions.hours: Box Office: Open on event days only, starting 3 hours prior to the scheduled event time. General Business Hours: Monday through Friday, 8:30 AM to 5:30 PM (for assistance/inquiries). Ticketmaster, Official Website
attractions.days_open: Event Days (typically open 3 hours prior to event time) Ticketmaster
attractions.admission: Prices vary significantly by event and seating tier; tickets are available through Ticketmaster and the official website. For instance, Spurs games and major concerts have dynamic pricing, while some community events may have different structures. No specific 'Free' tier mentioned for standard events. Ticketmaster, Official Website
highlights: Home of the 5-time NBA champion San Antonio Spurs Official Website
Hosts the annual San Antonio Stock Show & Rodeo every February Official Website
State-of-the-art scoreboard and fan experience amenities Wikipedia
Versatile seating capacity of up to 19,000 for concerts Wikipedia
Features 50 luxury suites and over 2,000 club seats Wikipedia
Newly renamed in 2023 following a naming rights agreement with Frost Bank NBA.com
attractions.tips: Arrive 60–90 minutes early for security and to explore concessions; the venue is completely cashless, so bring credit/debit cards or use mobile payment. Follow the clear bag policy to ensure entry. Pre-purchasing parking is highly recommended for high-demand events. Visit San Antonio, Official Website
attractions.nearby: Freeman Coliseum, San Antonio River Walk (approx. 3 miles), The Alamo (approx. 3 miles), San Antonio Stock Show & Rodeo Grounds. Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Government Canyon State Natural Area
attractions.description: Government Canyon State Natural Area is a 13,000-acre wilderness on the north side of San Antonio that protects a critical aquifer recharge zone for the city's drinking water. It offers a rugged Hill Country landscape with over 40 miles of trails for hiking and biking, ranging from gentle grasslands to remote canyon lands (Wikipedia, Texas Parks and Wildlife).
attractions.address: 12861 Galm Rd, San Antonio, TX 78254-9540
attractions.hours: Gate Hours: Friday - Monday: 7 a.m. – 10 p.m.; Tuesday - Thursday: 7 a.m. - 8 p.m. Office Hours: 8:30 a.m. - 4:30 p.m. (Texas Parks and Wildlife)
attractions.admission: Adult: $6 Daily, Child (12 Years and Under): Free (Texas Parks and Wildlife)
highlights: 110-million-year-old dinosaur tracks located along the Joe Johnston Route
Over 40 miles of hiking and mountain biking trails
Nesting habitat for the endangered Golden-cheeked Warbler and Black-capped Vireo
The historic Zizelmann House, a stone homestead built in the 1880s
Discovery Trail, an accessible 1.25-mile all-weather trail for families
attractions.tips: Reservations for both day use and camping are highly recommended as the park often reaches capacity. Check the park's social media or call ahead for trail status, as trails frequently close due to wet conditions to protect the sensitive ecosystem. Follow Leave No Trace principles and bring plenty of water, as many trails have limited shade (Texas Parks and Wildlife, Friends of Government Canyon).
attractions.nearby: The Alamo, San Antonio Missions National Historical Park, SeaWorld San Antonio, Six Flags Fiesta Texas, Landmark Inn State Historic Site (Texas Parks and Wildlife)

attractions.name: Gruene Historic District (Gruene Home)
attractions.description: Originally settled in the 1840s by German farmers, Gruene is a 15-acre National Historic District that offers a preserved snapshot of Texas culture and history (Gruene Home). Visitors can shop, dine, and dance within original 19th-century buildings, including the legendary Gruene Hall, Texas' oldest dance hall (Wikipedia, Travel + Leisure).
attractions.address: 1281 Gruene Rd, New Braunfels, TX 78130-3003 (Tripadvisor)
attractions.hours: Shops: Mon-Sun 10:00 AM - 6:00 PM; Restaurants & Live Music: Daily 11:00 AM - 10:00 PM or later; Gruene General Store: Sun-Thu 9:00 AM - 9:00 PM, Fri 9:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Sat 9:00 AM - 11:00 PM (Tripadvisor, Tripadvisor General Store)
attractions.admission: Free for general entry to the district (Gruene Market Days); Gruene Hall shows range from Free (daytime) to $12–$135+ for ticketed events (Gruene Special Events, Reddit)
highlights: Gruene Hall: The oldest continually operating dance hall in Texas, hosting live music daily (Gruene Hall).
Gristmill River Restaurant & Bar: An iconic eatery housed in a historic 1878 cotton gin overlooking the Guadalupe River (Travel + Leisure).
Gruene General Store: A top-rated Texas destination featuring a soda fountain and homemade fudge (Gruene Shop).
Old Gruene Market Days: A monthly event featuring nearly 100 artisans and food vendors (Gruene Market Days).
Guadalupe River: A scenic waterway popular for tubing, kayaking, and outdoor recreation (Airial Travel).
H.D. Gruene Mercantile: A historic landmark building from 1904 now serving as a modern retail hub (Gruene History).
attractions.tips: Arrive early on weekends to find parking, book Gruene Hall tickets in advance for popular shows, and try the famous 'fickle pickles' nearby (Big Chief Vacation Properties, Reddit).
attractions.nearby: Landa Park, Comal River, Schlitterbahn Waterpark, and the scenic River Road drive (Tripadvisor, Reddit)

attractions.name: Hildebrand Avenue Antiques District
attractions.description: Hildebrand Avenue is a historic thoroughfare in San Antonio renowned for its vibrant "Antique Row," featuring a high concentration of vintage shops and specialty boutiques. The area serves as a cultural bridge between the Monte Vista and Beacon Hill neighborhoods and is home to landmarks like the mysterious Miraflores Park and colorful murals. Visitors flock to the district for unique furniture, Mexican folk art, and a glimpse into the city's early 20th-century architectural heritage. (San Antonio Current, Visit San Antonio)
attractions.address: W. Hildebrand Ave (500-800 Blocks)
attractions.region: Monte Vista / Beacon Hill / Olmos Park
attractions.hours: Wed-Sat: 10:30 AM - 6:00 PM, Sun: 12:00 PM - 5:00 PM, Mon-Tue: Closed (R & G Antiques, Antiques on Hildebrand)
attractions.admission: Free (San Antonio Current)
highlights: Antiques District/Antique Row shopping for vintage furniture and decor
Miraflores Park, a historic sculpture garden with faux bois art
Selena Quintanilla Mural on the side of the Alamo Candy building
Proximity to Brackenridge Park and the San Antonio Zoo
Historic architecture of the surrounding Monte Vista and Beacon Hill neighborhoods
attractions.tips: Plan to visit between Wednesday and Sunday as many shops are closed on Mondays and Tuesdays. Budget at least a half-day to explore the various warehouses and smaller boutiques along the stretch. Parking is generally available on side streets or in small lots adjacent to the shops. (CultureMap San Antonio)
attractions.nearby: San Antonio Zoo, Brackenridge Park, The DoSeum, Japanese Tea Garden, Trinity University (Wikipedia, Visit San Antonio)

attractions.name: Historic Market Square
attractions.description: Historic Market Square is a vibrant three-block outdoor plaza in San Antonio, famously described as the largest Mexican market outside of Mexico. It features over 100 locally owned shops and stalls, authentic Mexican restaurants, and a festive atmosphere with live entertainment and working artists celebrating Hispanic culture (City of San Antonio, Market Square Official Site).
attractions.address: 514 W Commerce St, San Antonio, TX 78207
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: 10:00 AM - 6:00 PM (Note: Mi Tierra Cafe & Bakery is open 24 hours; kitchens in the Farmer's Market may close at 5:00 PM) (Market Square Official Site)
attractions.admission: Free (City of San Antonio)
highlights: Over 100 locally owned shops and stalls selling authentic Mexican goods and artifacts (City of San Antonio)
Iconic restaurants like Mi Tierra Cafe & Bakery (open 24 hours) and La Margarita Mexican Restaurant (Market Square Official Site)
Live music and cultural performances nearly every weekend (Market Square Official Site)
Working artists demonstrating traditional crafts like pottery and leatherwork (Market Square Official Site)
Centro de Artes gallery showcasing Latinx art and culture (Tripadvisor)
Festive celebrations for holidays like Cinco de Mayo and Día de los Muertos (Market Square Official Site)
attractions.tips: Visit on weekends for the most lively atmosphere and live entertainment. Be sure to stop by Mi Tierra's bakery for traditional pan dulce. Wear comfortable walking shoes to explore the three-block pedestrian mall (Reddit, Market Square Official Site).
attractions.nearby: Milam Park, San Antonio River Walk, The Alamo, UTSA Downtown Campus (Market Square Official Site, Visit San Antonio)

attractions.name: Historic Market Square (El Mercado) (Official Website)
attractions.description: Historic Market Square is a vibrant three-block outdoor plaza and pedestrian mall in downtown San Antonio, famously known as the largest Mexican market in the United States. It features over 100 locally owned shops and stalls offering authentic Mexican crafts, hand-crafted leather goods, and traditional Tex-Mex cuisine across its festive indoor and outdoor areas. The square is a hub of cultural activity, hosting numerous festivals and live entertainment throughout the year (Official Website, Wikipedia)).
attractions.address: 514 W Commerce St, San Antonio, TX 78207-8102 (Official Website, Tripadvisor)
attractions.hours: Monday: 10 a.m. - 6 p.m., Tuesday: 10 a.m. - 6 p.m., Wednesday: 10 a.m. - 6 p.m., Thursday: 10 a.m. - 6 p.m., Friday: 10 a.m. - 6 p.m., Saturday: 10 a.m. - 6 p.m., Sunday: 10 a.m. - 6 p.m. (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Free (Airial Travel)
highlights: Over 100 locally owned shops and stalls (Official Website)
Authentic Mexican and Tex-Mex cuisine at iconic spots like Mi Tierra Café & Bakery and La Margarita (Official Website)
Cultural festivities including Cinco de Mayo and Día de los Muertos celebrations (Official Website)
Live music and street performances nearly every weekend (Official Website)
Centro de Artes, an on-site art gallery dedicated to Latino arts and culture (Tripadvisor)
A wide array of Mexican artifacts, apparel, and hand-crafted leather goods (Official Website)
attractions.tips: Visit on weekends to enjoy live music and a festive atmosphere, though it will be more crowded (Tripadvisor). Don't miss the 24-hour Mi Tierra Café & Bakery for its famous Mexican pastries and vibrant murals (Reddit). Parking is available in nearby lots, usually costing around $10 (Airial Travel).
attractions.nearby: San Fernando De Bexar Cathedral, Spanish Governor's Palace, Milam Park, San Antonio River Walk, The Alamo, Casa Navarro State Historic Site (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Houston Street San Antonio
attractions.category: Entertainment and Commercial District / Historic Landmark
attractions.description: Houston Street is one of San Antonio's oldest and most popular thoroughfares, serving as a major pedestrian and commercial district in the heart of the central business district. It is lined with historic theaters, retail shops, and trendy restaurants, connecting iconic landmarks like the Alamo to the San Pedro Creek Culture Park. (Wikipedia), Hotels.com)
attractions.address: E. Houston St (from Alamo Plaza to Santa Rosa St)
attractions.hours: 24/7 (Public access; business hours typically 10:00 AM - 10:00 PM) (Hotels.com, The Palm)
attractions.admission: Free (Public street; individual attractions like Majestic Theatre or Buckhorn Museum have their own fees) (Hotels.com, Visit San Antonio)
highlights: Historic Majestic and Empire Theatres (Old Town Trolley Tours)
The Buckhorn Saloon & Texas Ranger Museum (Hotels.com)
'Interwoven' colorful mural at Navarro Street (Hotels.com)
Holidays on Houston Street festive celebration (onhoustonstreet.com)
Proximity to the Alamo and River Walk (Wikipedia))
Access to San Pedro Creek Culture Park (Hotels.com)
attractions.tips: The street is very walkable, making it ideal for strolling between the Alamo and nearby theaters. For a local treat, start your day with coffee at La Panadería. (Hotels.com)
attractions.nearby: The Alamo, San Antonio River Walk, San Fernando Cathedral, Main Plaza, San Pedro Creek Culture Park (Wikipedia), Old Town Trolley Tours)

attractions.name: Instituto Cultural de México en San Antonio
attractions.description: The Instituto Cultural de México en San Antonio is the permanent cultural representation of the Mexican government in the city. It promotes Mexico's rich heritage and contemporary creative vitality through a diverse range of exhibitions, performances, and educational activities. Official Website, San Antonio Report
attractions.address: 600 Hemisfair Plaza Way, San Antonio, TX 78205 Texas Time Travel, San Antonio Report
attractions.hours: Monday - Friday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Saturday - Sunday: Closed Official Website, Tripadvisor
attractions.days_open: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday Official Website
attractions.admission: Free Tripadvisor, Yelp
highlights: Located in the original Mexican Pavilion from the 1968 HemisFair. Texas Archive
Features three distinct art galleries showcasing rotating exhibitions of Mexican and local artists. Texas Time Travel
Houses a 250-seat auditorium for performances, film screenings, and lectures. San Antonio Report
Offers a multi-purpose room for workshops and community engagement activities. Texas Historical Commission
Serves as a key cultural link between the Mexican government and the San Antonio community. Official Website
attractions.tips: Admission is free, but visitors are typically required to register at the front desk. For convenient access, park at the Tower of the Americas parking lot, which is a short walk away. Yelp, San Antonio Report
attractions.nearby: Tower of the Americas, Yanaguana Garden at Hemisfair, Henry B. González Convention Center, San Antonio River Walk, The Alamo Tripadvisor

attractions.name: La Villita Historic Arts Village
attractions.category: Historic Arts Village / Tourist Attraction Official Website, Wikipedia
attractions.description: La Villita Historic Arts Village is a vibrant art community and historic district located on the south bank of the San Antonio River Walk. Originally one of the city's first neighborhoods, it now houses over 25 galleries, boutiques, and restaurants featuring local handcrafted art, jewelry, and pottery. Official Website, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 418 Villita St., San Antonio, TX 78205 Official Website
attractions.region: Downtown / South Bank of the San Antonio River Wikipedia
attractions.hours: Monday - Saturday: 10:00 AM - 6:00 PM; Sunday: 11:00 AM - 4:00 PM Official Website
attractions.admission: Free Official Website, Tripadvisor
highlights: Artisans and galleries specializing in metal smithing, pottery, and painting Official Website
Arneson River Theatre, an outdoor performance venue on the River Walk Wikipedia
Cos House, a historic site where the Articles of Capitulation were signed in 1835 Wikipedia
Little Church of La Villita, a historic non-denominational church Wikipedia
Annual host of 'A Night in Old San Antonio' (NIOSA) during Fiesta Wikipedia
Historic cobblestone streets and tree-shaded courtyards Tripadvisor
attractions.tips: There is no onsite parking, so use nearby downtown parking garages; the village is best explored on foot and is directly accessible from the River Walk via the Arneson River Theatre. Official Website, Hotels.com
attractions.nearby: San Antonio River Walk, The Alamo, Hemisfair Park, Briscoe Western Art Museum, Henry B. Gonzalez Convention Center Wikipedia, Official Website

attractions.name: Luminaria Contemporary Arts Festival
attractions.description: The Luminaria Contemporary Arts Festival is San Antonio’s premier multi-disciplinary arts event, transforming public spaces into a vibrant nighttime gallery. It features over 200 artists across various genres, including live music, digital art, film, and immersive installations. The festival is a free, all-ages celebration of local and international creative expression, designed to engage the community with innovative art in historic settings. Luminaria Official Site, San Antonio Report
attractions.address: 126 Gonzales St (St. Paul Square)
attractions.hours: Monday-Friday: Closed; Saturday: 6:00 PM - 12:00 AM (Main Event); Sunday: Closed Luminaria Official Site, Get Creative San Antonio
attractions.days_open: Typically one Saturday in October (e.g., October 18, 2025) Luminaria Official Site
attractions.admission: Free (General Admission); VIP tickets available for approximately $100 Luminaria Official Site, St. Paul Square Blog
highlights: Immersive art installations and large-scale sculptures across historic streets
Live music performances featuring various genres and tribute acts
Digital art, laser displays, and multi-media film screenings
Performance art including dance, cirque, and aerial acrobatics
Poetry readings and spoken word by local and state laureates
Interactive creative experiences allowing visitors to engage directly with artists
attractions.tips: Use the VIA Transit app for public transportation routes and schedules. Parking is available at the Alamodome, Hemisfair, or Rivercenter Mall garages, though ridesharing is recommended due to limited downtown parking. Wear comfortable walking shoes as the festival spans several city blocks in an open-air setting. Luminaria Official Site, Yelp
attractions.nearby: Alamodome, Hemisfair Park, San Antonio Riverwalk, Henry B. Gonzalez Convention Center, Rivercenter Mall Luminaria Official Site, Visit San Antonio

attractions.name: McNay Art Museum
attractions.description: Built in the 1920s by artist and educator Marion Koogler McNay, this Spanish Colonial Revival residence became Texas’ first modern art museum in 1954. The museum engages a diverse community through 19th and 20th-century European and American art, spanning 25 acres of beautifully landscaped sculpture grounds. Official Site, Tripadvisor
attractions.address: 6000 N New Braunfels Ave, San Antonio, TX 78209-4618
attractions.hours: Monday: Closed; Tuesday: 10 AM - 4 PM; Wednesday: 10 AM - 6 PM; Thursday: 10 AM - 9 PM; Friday: 10 AM - 6 PM; Saturday: 10 AM - 5 PM; Sunday: Noon - 5 PM. Official Site
attractions.admission: Adults: $23; Teens (13-19): $10; Seniors (65+): $18; Students (20+ with ID): $18; McNay Members: FREE; Children (12 & Under): FREE; Military & Veterans: FREE; SNAP, WIC, & MAP Recipients: FREE. H-E-B Free Thursday Nights (4-9 PM) and First Sundays (Noon-5 PM) offer free general admission ($10 fee for special exhibitions). Official Site
highlights: Renowned collection of modern masters including Picasso, Gauguin, Matisse, and O'Keeffe Visit San Antonio
Stunning Spanish Colonial Revival-style mansion with hand-painted ceilings and decorative tiles Official Site
25-acre sculpture garden featuring works by Robert Indiana, Willie Cole, and Luis A. Jiménez Jr. Tripadvisor
Tobin Collection of Theatre Arts featuring scenic and costume designs Official Site
Rotating special exhibitions like 'untitled: 20 Years of Collecting Contemporary Art' Official Site
attractions.tips: Visit during H-E-B Free Thursday Nights (4-9 PM) or the first Sunday of the month for free general admission. Allow time to explore the 25-acre grounds and sculpture garden, which are also popular for photography and picnics. Official Site, Reddit
attractions.nearby: Witte Museum, San Antonio Museum of Art, The DoSeum, Japanese Tea Garden, San Antonio Botanical Garden, and Tycoon Flats. Tripadvisor, Reddit

attractions.name: Medina Lake (Bandera County Park at Medina Lake)
attractions.description: Medina Lake is a scenic reservoir on the Medina River in the Texas Hill Country, created by the historic Medina Dam in 1913. Spanning over 5,000 acres, it serves as a major recreational hub for the San Antonio area, offering opportunities for boating, fishing, and swimming. The lake is known for its clear waters, limestone bluffs, and role as a critical irrigation source for local farmlands.
attractions.address: 14444 Park Road 37
attractions.hours: Memorial Day to Labor Day: 8:00 AM - 8:00 PM (Daily); September to May: 8:00 AM - 8:00 PM (Friday - Sunday). No admittance after 7:00 PM.
attractions.days_open: Seasonal: Open 7 days a week (Memorial Day to Labor Day); Open Friday-Sunday only (September to May).
attractions.admission: $5.00 per person (county residents), $10.00 per person (out-of-county residents), Free for children under 6 years of age. Cash only. Additional fees for boats/jet skis ($10.00).
highlights: Historic Medina Dam, a National Register of Historic Places listing and Texas Historic Civil Engineering Landmark.
Recreational activities including fishing (largemouth bass, catfish), boating, jet skiing, and swimming.
Picturesque Texas Hill Country scenery with 110 miles of shoreline and limestone bluffs.
Designated swimming areas and picnic spots with charcoal grills for family outings.
Wildlife viewing opportunities, including local bird species and migratory waterfowl.
Boat launch ramps accessible at varying water levels, with 24-hour access at some locations.
attractions.tips: The lake is currently experiencing historically low water levels due to drought; check current conditions before visiting. Arrive early on weekends as the park operates on a first-come, first-served basis and can reach capacity. Payments must be made in cash, and pets are welcome but must remain on a leash at all times. Bring your own supplies, as the area is primarily for day-use with limited immediate commercial facilities.

attractions.name: Mission San José y San Miguel de Aguayo
attractions.description: Established in 1720 and known as the 'Queen of the Missions,' Mission San José is the largest of the San Antonio missions and a UNESCO World Heritage site. It serves as a premier example of Spanish colonial architecture and was extensively restored in the 1930s to show its original design. Today, it remains an active Catholic parish and the heart of the San Antonio Missions National Historical Park.
attractions.address: 6701 San José Drive
attractions.hours: Visitor Center & Buildings: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Grounds: Sunrise to Sunset NPS
attractions.days_open: Daily (Closed Thanksgiving, Christmas, and New Year's Day) NPS
attractions.admission: Free NPS, Visit San Antonio
highlights: The Rose Window, a masterpiece of Spanish Colonial ornamentation NPS
The Grist Mill, which was the first of its kind in Texas NPS
The Church, featuring intricate Baroque carvings and a hand-carved spiral stairway Hoteles.com
Restored defensive walls and Indigenous family quarters NPS
Free 20-minute orientation film 'Gente de Razón' in the Visitor Center Visit San Antonio
Daily ranger-led tours starting at the Visitor Center NPS
attractions.tips: Start at the Visitor Center to watch the orientation film and pick up a map. Plan for 1.5 to 2 hours to explore the grounds, and wear comfortable walking shoes for uneven stone paths. Visit in the morning to avoid the afternoon heat and join one of the free guided ranger tours typically offered at 10 AM and 11 AM. As it is an active parish, please be respectful of ongoing services. NPS, Hoteles.com

attractions.name: Mission San Juan Capistrano (also known as Mission San Juan)
attractions.description: Mission San Juan Capistrano is a historic Spanish colonial mission and part of the San Antonio Missions National Historical Park, a UNESCO World Heritage site. Originally founded in 1716 in East Texas, it was relocated in 1731 to the banks of the San Antonio River and became known for its fertile farmlands and self-sustainable community. Today, it remains an active Catholic parish and features a chapel, bell tower, and a demonstration farm. (National Park Service, Visit San Antonio)

attractions.days_open: Daily (except Thanksgiving, Christmas, and New Year's Day) (National Park Service)
attractions.admission: Free (National Park Service, Visit San Antonio)
highlights: UNESCO World Heritage site designation (Wikipedia))
Historic acequias and active demonstration farm (National Park Service)
Yanaguana Trail, a paved nature loop leading to the San Antonio River (National Park Service)
Active Catholic parish with an original chapel and bell tower still in use (Visit San Antonio)
Remnants of an unfinished larger stone church from 1772 (Wikipedia))
Part of the San Antonio Missions National Historical Park Hike & Bike Trail (National Park Service)
attractions.tips: Start your visit at the Mission San José Visitor Center for an orientation film and maps. Wear comfortable walking shoes and sunscreen as much of the site is outdoors. Bicycles are allowed on the trails but must be walked on sidewalks inside the mission grounds. (National Park Service, Visit San Antonio)
attractions.nearby: Mission San José, Mission Espada, San Antonio River Walk, Mission Espada Aqueduct (National Park Service, Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Monte Vista Historic District
attractions.description: The Monte Vista Historic District is one of the largest and best-preserved historic neighborhoods in the United States, featuring homes built during San Antonio's "Gilded Age" between 1890 and 1930. The district is renowned for its diverse architectural styles, including Queen Anne, Tudor, and Mediterranean, and was once the preferred residence for the city's prominent oil and cattle barons. (Monte Vista Historical Association, Wikipedia)
attractions.address: 233 Bushnell Avenue
attractions.hours: Mon-Tue: 11:00 AM - 8:00 PM; Wed-Sun: 10:00 AM - 6:00 PM (Landa Library hours; the district itself is a public neighborhood accessible 24/7) (San Antonio Public Library)
attractions.admission: Free (Cassey Golden Photography)
highlights: Features a diverse range of architectural styles including Queen Anne, Tudor, and Hollywood Bungalow (Monte Vista Historical Association)
Home to the Hannah Landa Memorial Library, housed in a stunning 1920s mansion (Wikipedia)
Designated as a local historic district in 1975 and listed on the National Register of Historic Places in 1998 (Wikipedia)
Contains the Landa Gardens, which offer a playground, walking paths, and beautifully landscaped grounds (Landa Gardens Conservancy)
Located in Midtown, providing a peaceful escape within the heart of the city (Monte Vista Historical Association)
Bordered by Trinity University and located near San Pedro Springs Park (Monte Vista Historical Association)
attractions.tips: Walking or driving through the neighborhood is highly recommended to appreciate the unique architecture of the historic homes. Visitors should also see the Selena mural on the side of the Alamo Candy Company building and enjoy a peaceful stroll through the Landa Library gardens. Note that while photography is free on the library grounds, a permit is required for professional sessions. (YouTube Tour, Kelly Williams Photographer)
