attractions.description: The University of Texas at San Antonio (UTSA) is a prominent public research university and the largest institution of higher education in San Antonio. Founded in 1969, it is recognized as a Tier One research university, offering over 350 academic programs to more than 42,000 students.
attractions.address: One UTSA Circle, San Antonio, TX 78249
attractions.region: Northwest San Antonio (Main Campus) / Downtown (Southwest/Downtown Campus)
attractions.hours: Monday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Tuesday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Wednesday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Thursday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Friday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Saturday: Closed, Sunday: Closed (Welcome Center hours). Campus grounds are generally accessible 24/7.
attractions.admission: Free for campus visits; some events or specific facilities may require tickets. Visitor parking is $2.50/hour.
highlights: Classified as an R1 Doctoral University with 'Very High Research Activity' by the Carnegie Foundation.
Third-largest public research university in Texas by annual research expenditures.
Member of the Association of Universities for Research in Astronomy (AURA), operating world-class observatories.
Home to the Curtis Vaughan Jr. Observatory for astronomical research and public viewing.
Manages the Institute of Texan Cultures, which preserves the state's diverse cultural history.
Features the 'Birds Up' hand signal and Rowdy the Roadrunner as central campus traditions.
attractions.tips: Visitors should park in the Bauerle Road Garage for the easiest access to the Main Campus Welcome Center. It is recommended to download the UTSA self-guided tour handout or use the interactive online campus map to navigate the expansive 600-acre Main Campus. Prospective students should register for official tours in advance through the Future Roadrunner portal.
attractions.nearby: The Shops at La Cantera, Six Flags Fiesta Texas, The Block SA (Food Truck Park), Santikos Palladium IMAX, San Antonio River Walk (Downtown Campus area), The Alamo (Downtown).

attractions.name: The Witte Museum
attractions.description: The Witte Museum is San Antonio's premier institution where nature, science, and culture meet, dedicated to telling the stories of Texas from prehistory to the present. Located on the banks of the San Antonio River, it features a diverse collection of dinosaur bones, historic artifacts, Texas art, and interactive science exhibits. The Witte Museum
attractions.address: 3801 Broadway, San Antonio, Texas 78209
attractions.hours: Monday: 10 a.m. – 5 p.m.; Tuesday: 10 a.m. – 6 p.m.; Wednesday – Saturday: 10 a.m. – 5 p.m.; Sunday: Noon – 5 p.m. (Members-only hour: 11 a.m. – Noon). Official Website
attractions.admission: Adults (19-64): $17.00, Seniors (65+): $16.00, Teens (13-18): $16.00, Children (4-12): $11.00, Children (3 & under): Free. Bexar County residents enjoy free General Admission on Tuesdays from 3:00 PM to 6:00 PM. Official Website
highlights: Naylor Family Dinosaur Gallery: Features massive skeletons and interactive displays from the Cretaceous Period.
McLean Family Texas Wild Gallery: Showcases over 150 animals in dioramas representing Texas' diverse ecosystems.
Robert J. & Helen C. Kleberg South Texas Heritage Center: Explores Texas history from the frontier era to the present.
Kittie West Nelson Ferguson People of the Pecos Gallery: Details the 9,000-year history of hunter-gatherers in the Lower Pecos.
H-E-B Body Adventure: A four-story interactive health and wellness experience.
Zachry Family Acequia Garden: Beautiful outdoor spaces along the San Antonio River featuring historic water systems.
attractions.tips: Parking is free in the Brackenridge Park Parking Garage on Avenue B. Plan for 2-3 hours to explore the full campus, and consider visiting during Free Tuesdays if you are a Bexar County resident. Photography is encouraged but flash and tripods are not permitted. Official Website
attractions.nearby: Brackenridge Park, San Antonio Zoo, San Antonio Botanical Garden, The Pearl District, DoSeum, and Japanese Tea Garden. Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Tower of the Americas
attractions.description: The Tower of the Americas is an iconic 750-foot tall observation tower and restaurant located in downtown San Antonio. Built as the centerpiece for the 1968 World's Fair, it offers breathtaking 360-degree panoramic views of the Alamo City and features a revolving restaurant, 4D theater ride, and a lounge. Official Website, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 739 E. Cesar E. Chavez Blvd, San Antonio, TX 78205
attractions.hours: Retail, Tower Café, Observation Deck & 4D Theater: Sun–Thu 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Fri–Sat 10:00 AM – 11:00 PM; Chart House Restaurant: Sun–Thu 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Fri–Sat 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM Official Website
attractions.admission: Adults: $19.50, Seniors (65+): $16.00, Military: $16.00, Children (4–12): $15.00, Children 3 & under: Free (Observation Deck and 4D Theater admission) Official Website
highlights: Flags Over Texas Observation Deck offering panoramic city views
Skies Over Texas 4D Theater Ride immersive experience
Chart House revolving seafood and steak restaurant
Bar 601 and Tower Café for elevated drinks and bites
Interactive floor maps and historic photos documenting Texas history
Towercade featuring classic and modern arcade games
attractions.tips: Visit at sunset for the best views as the city lights come on. Reservations for the Chart House restaurant are highly recommended and allow you to bypass the elevator fee for the observation deck. Weekdays are generally less crowded than weekends. Note that the entire property went cashless starting February 1, 2026. Official Website FAQ, Reddit
attractions.nearby: Hemisfair Park, San Antonio River Walk, The Alamo, Henry B. Gonzalez Convention Center, Institute of Texan Cultures, La Villita Historic Arts Village Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Trinity University
attractions.description: Trinity University is a private liberal arts college in San Antonio, Texas, known for its distinctive red-brick architecture and 125-acre Skyline Campus. Founded in 1869, it offers a transformative education rooted in the liberal arts and sciences with a strong emphasis on student experience and community engagement.
attractions.address: 1 Trinity Place, San Antonio, TX 78212-7200
attractions.region: Midtown / Monte Vista Historic District Wikipedia
attractions.hours: Campus Grounds: Open 24/7; Admissions Office: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm, Saturday Varies, Sunday Closed Trinity University Office Hours
attractions.days_open: Monday-Sunday (Campus grounds); Monday-Friday (Offices) Trinity University Office Hours
attractions.admission: Free (Publicly accessible campus grounds, lectures, and events vary) Trinity University Visitors Info
highlights: Murchison Tower, a 166-foot landmark offering views of downtown San Antonio.
125-acre Skyline Campus, designated a National Historic Landmark District.
Red-brick architecture modeled after an Italian village by O'Neil Ford.
Proximity to Brackenridge Park and the San Antonio Zoo.
Home to the Trinity Tigers and a diverse student body from 47 states and 58 countries.
Distinguished Lecture Series bringing world-class speakers to campus.
attractions.tips: Take a virtual 360-degree tour before visiting; parking is available on campus with specific policies; hop on the VIVA culture bus for easy access to downtown. Trinity University Visitors Info
attractions.nearby: Brackenridge Park, San Antonio Zoo, The Pearl, San Antonio Museum of Art, The Alamo Trinity University Play in San Antonio

attractions.name: University of the Incarnate Word
attractions.description: The University of the Incarnate Word is a private Catholic university founded in 1881 by the Sisters of Charity of the Incarnate Word. It is the largest Catholic university in Texas and ranks among the top schools in the nation for graduating Hispanic students. The campus offers over 150 academic programs and is a leader in transformational education. (UIW About, Wikipedia)
attractions.address: 4301 Broadway, San Antonio, TX 78209 (UIW Visitor Info)
attractions.hours: Office of Admissions: Monday-Friday 8 a.m. – 5 p.m., First Saturday of each month 9 a.m. – 1 p.m.; Library Visitor Hours: Monday-Thursday 8 a.m. – 10 p.m., Friday 8 a.m. – 5 p.m., Saturday 10 a.m. – 4 p.m., Sunday 2 p.m. – 10 p.m. (UIW Visitor Info, UIW Library)
attractions.admission: Free (guided tours by appointment) (UIW Visit)
highlights: Largest Catholic university in Texas (UIW Home)
Top-ranked institution for military veterans (UIW Home)
Founded in 1881 with a rich history of serving the San Antonio community (Wikipedia)
Home to the Division I Incarnate Word Cardinals athletic program (Wikipedia)
Beautiful 154-acre main campus located in the heart of San Antonio (Wikipedia)
attractions.tips: Visitors can obtain a free guest parking permit online via the T2 Cardinal Cars website; guided campus tours are available by appointment, while self-guided tour booklets can be picked up at the Office of Admissions. (UIW Visitor Info)
attractions.nearby: San Antonio Zoo, Witte Museum, Brackenridge Park, Japanese Tea Gardens, San Antonio Botanical Garden (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: UTSA Roadrunners (University of Texas at San Antonio Athletics)
attractions.description: The UTSA Roadrunners is the intercollegiate athletic program of the University of Texas at San Antonio, competing in NCAA Division I as a member of the American Athletic Conference. The program features 17 varsity sports and is renowned for its rapidly rising football program and state-of-the-art training facilities like the Roadrunner Athletics Center of Excellence.
attractions.address: One UTSA Circle
attractions.hours: Ticket Office: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM (Monday – Friday); Event times vary by schedule.
attractions.days_open: Ticket Office: Monday – Friday; Events: Seasonal (Game days vary)
attractions.admission: UTSA Students: Free; Free Sports: Cross Country, Golf, Tennis, Track & Field; Football Single Tickets: ~$24 - $116+; Student Guest Tickets: $10; Other sports (Basketball, Baseball, etc.): Varies by game.
highlights: Competes in the NCAA Division I American Athletic Conference (AAC) after a successful stint in Conference USA.
Home football games are played at the 65,000-seat Alamodome, creating one of the most electric atmospheres in San Antonio.
Features the $40.4 million Roadrunner Athletics Center of Excellence (RACE), a premier training and academic facility.
The UTSA Cheer team has secured multiple National Cheerleading Associations (NCA) collegiate national championships.
Boasts a passionate fan culture centered around the "Birds Up" hand sign and the "210" area code pride.
attractions.tips: Arrive 90 minutes to 2 hours early for home football games at the Alamodome to experience the legendary tailgating and avoid entrance delays. Use the official UTSA Roadrunners mobile app for seamless ticket management and stadium information.
attractions.nearby: Six Flags Fiesta Texas, The Shops at La Cantera, Topgolf San Antonio, Andretti Indoor Karting & Games

attractions.name: Yanaguana Garden at Hemisfair (Hemisfair)
attractions.description: Yanaguana Garden at Hemisfair is a 4.1-acre urban wonderland in downtown San Antonio that has been open to the public since 2015 (Hemisfair). It features a variety of active play areas, inclusive playground equipment, and public art installations designed for visitors of all ages and abilities (Landscape Performance Series). The park serves as a vibrant community hub that combines modern recreation with historic preservation within the Hemisfair district (Visit San Antonio).
attractions.address: 434 S. Alamo St. (Hemisfair, Tripadvisor)
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: 5:00 AM - 12:00 AM (Hemisfair)
attractions.admission: Free (Hemisfair, Tripadvisor)
highlights: Inclusive Playground: Features climbing structures and swings designed for all ability levels (Hemisfair).
Union Pacific Railroad Splash Pad: A popular seasonal water feature for cooling off (Hemisfair).
Central Games Plaza: Offers oversized checkers and chess boards for public use (Hemisfair).
Public Art Installations: Includes the "Pantera Azul" mosaic and other works by local artists (Landscape Performance Series).
Kelleher Family Lawn: A scenic green space ideal for picnics and community gatherings (Hemisfair).
Sand Play Area: Equipped with a hand-water pump and sand digger for creative play (Hemisfair).
attractions.tips: The Hemisfair Garage offers the first 30 minutes of parking for free, with hourly rates thereafter (Hemisfair). While bicycles and scooters are encouraged for transportation, they are not permitted on sidewalks inside the parks; bike racks are provided throughout the area (Hemisfair). The splash pad typically operates daily from 9:00 AM to 9:00 PM during the season (City of San Antonio).
attractions.nearby: Tower of the Americas, The Alamo, San Antonio River Walk, La Villita Historic Arts Village, Magik Children's Theatre (Hemisfair, Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Yanaguana Trail at Mission San Juan
attractions.description: The Yanaguana Trail is a 0.3-mile paved nature loop that winds through lush riparian woodland along an original channel of the San Antonio River. This ADA-accessible path serves as a peaceful 'oasis in the city,' showcasing the native Texas ecosystems that existed before the arrival of Spanish missionaries. National Park Service
attractions.address: 9101 Graf Road
attractions.region: Berg's Mill / Mission Reach National Park Service
attractions.hours: Grounds: Sunrise to Sunset; Visitor hours: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM National Park Service
attractions.days_open: Daily (Closed Thanksgiving, Christmas, and New Year's Day) National Park Service
attractions.admission: Free National Park Service
highlights: 0.3-mile paved, ADA-accessible loop trail through a lush riparian area.
Follows an original, preserved channel of the San Antonio River.
Abundant wildlife including turtles, owls, Great Blue Herons, and snakes.
Native plant life such as pecan and mulberry trees and grape vines.
Part of the UNESCO World Heritage San Antonio Missions National Historical Park.
Connects to the historic living demonstration farm at Mission San Juan Capistrano.
attractions.tips: Visit in the early morning to avoid the intense Texas summer heat and observe more active wildlife. The trail is a short, easy walk that can be combined with a tour of the Mission San Juan church and the historic acequia-fed demonstration farm. Visit San Antonio
attractions.nearby: Mission San Juan Capistrano (on-site), Mission San José, Mission Espada, San Antonio River Walk (Mission Reach section), Espada Dam and Aqueduct National Park Service
